V.description: Final velocity of an object after free falling a vertical distance h under constant gravitational acceleration g

g.description: Acceleration due to gravity at the location of the fall

h.description: Height or vertical displacement traversed during the free fall

applicable_scope: Valid for free fall near planetary surfaces where atmospheric resistance and rotational effects are neglected; assumes constant gravity over the fall distance and initial velocity of zero.

formula_name: 旅行时间公式（单位转换形式）Travel Time Formula (with Unit Conversion)

Time.description: Travel time, originally given in hours; convert to seconds for SI

D.description: One-way distance to the target (in kilometers)

applicable_scope: Estimates two-way signal or light travel time for planetary distances, based on a fixed factor (here, 8.5 assumed as km/min), before converting unit to hours (and SI). Primarily for rough planning in interplanetary communication or travel assuming constant speed; actual physics may vary with detail.

formula_name: 旅行推销员问题 Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP)

d_{i,j}.description: Euclidean distance between point i and point j in a two-dimensional Cartesian space

x_i.description: x-coordinate of the i-th star/object in the catalog

x_j.description: x-coordinate of the j-th star/object in the catalog

y_i.description: y-coordinate of the i-th star/object in the catalog

y_j.description: y-coordinate of the j-th star/object in the catalog

n.description: Number of nodes or stellar objects to visit

constraints: All (x_i, y_i) for i=1,...,n are defined and distinct
applicable_scope: Applicable to optimization of path routing for visiting or measuring multiple stars or objects within a stellar field, requiring minimization of total travel distance under local Euclidean geometry. Theoretical basis is in combinatorial optimization; physical constraints may include instrument travel limits or observation scheduling.

formula_name: 脉冲星周期线性公式 Pulsar Period Linear Formula

P.description: Observed pulsar period

k.description: Linear growth rate of the period, representing spin-down rate

applicable_scope: Applicable to pulsars demonstrating a linear increase in rotation period over time due to electromagnetic slowing; best suited for slow spin-down scenarios and initial estimates over moderately short timescales.

formula_name: 幂律分布 Power-law Distribution

F(x).description: Quantity following a power-law dependence on x

x.unit: Depends on context (e.g., m, kg, s)
x.description: Independent variable, often spatial, mass, or luminosity scale

a.unit: Depends on F(x) and n (so that F(x) has correct SI units)
a.description: Normalization coefficient, sets the scale of the distribution

n.description: Power-law index, determines the steepness of the distribution

applicable_scope: Applies to systems exhibiting scale-free or self-similar distributions, such as luminosity, mass, or spatial distributions in galactic astronomy. Assumes the regime where power-law behavior is physically justified, such as observed for star clusters, interstellar medium structures, and certain dynamical processes.

formula_name: 面积比例法则 Area Proportionality Formula

Delta F.description: Drop in observed flux due to planetary transit

applicable_scope: Used to estimate the planet-to-star radius ratio from transit light curve observations; assumes the planet fully transits the stellar disk, the star is much larger than the planet, and limb darkening is negligible.

formula_name: 秒差距到光年转换公式 Parsec to Light Year Conversion Formula

L.description: Distance in meters corresponding to light years

P.description: Distance in meters corresponding to parsecs

applicable_scope: Conversion between parsec and light year units for astronomical distances; assumes standard definitions (1 parsec ≈ 3.26 light years). Applicable to extragalactic and interstellar distance measurement in galactic astronomy.

formula_name: 秒换算成年公式 Seconds to Years Conversion Formula
applicable_scope: Conversion between time in seconds and years, applicable for astronomical timescales. Assumes 1 year ≈ 3.1×10⁷ seconds; not suitable for high-precision calendrical calculations.

formula_name: 秒与年的换算公式 Seconds-to-Years Conversion Formula

year.description: One Julian year, used as standard astronomical time interval

applicable_scope: Standard astronomical and physical calculations requiring conversion between years and seconds; assumes a Julian year (365.25 days). Not suitable for precise calendar computation or relativistic time-dilation scenarios.

formula_name: 摩尔数公式 Number of Moles Formula

n.description: Number of moles of the substance

M.description: Molar mass of the substance

applicable_scope: Applicable to the calculation of the number of moles for any chemical species in planetary atmospheres, surfaces, or interiors where the mass and molar mass are known and chemical compositions are well-defined.

formula_name: 莫尔魏德投影 Mollweide Projection

x.description: Normalized x-coordinate on the Mollweide projection map

y.description: Normalized y-coordinate on the Mollweide projection map

lambda.description: Longitude of the point to be projected

applicable_scope: Used for mapping spherical astronomical coordinate systems (e.g., Galactic longitude and latitude) onto a 2D plane. It preserves area and is commonly employed in sky maps or Galactic astronomy for visualization, not for kinematic or physical computation.

formula_name: 能量分配公式 Energy Partition Formula
latex: E_{\text{flare (available)}} = E_{\text{radiation}} + E_{\text{CME}} + E_{\text{heating}} + E_{\text{gravity lost}}

E_{\\text{flare (available)}}.description: Total energy available during a stellar flare event

E_{\\text{CME}}.description: Kinetic energy carried away by Coronal Mass Ejection (CME)

formula_name: 能量公式 Energy Formula

formula_name: 能量换算公式 Energy Conversion Formula

E.description: Energy released by a stellar event or source

E_{bomb}.description: Energy released by one bomb (reference explosion), typically 4.184 \times 10^{12} J for one kiloton of TNT

applicable_scope: Conversion of large energy scales typical of stellar phenomena into more comprehensible units by referencing the energy of one explosion event; useful in pedagogical and comparative contexts only, not fundamental physics.

formula_name: 能量释放分数公式 Energy Output Fraction Formula

m.description: Mass involved in the energy conversion process, typically accreted mass

\\eta.description: Energy conversion efficiency; for accretion onto a non-rotating black hole, typically taken as 0.07

formula_name: 能量守恒定律 Law of Conservation of Energy

formula_name: 能量守恒-数量推导公式 Number of Particles Formula (Total/Individual)

L_{\\text{total}}.description: Total luminosity or energy output of the system

L_{\\text{grain}}.description: Luminosity or energy output of a single particle or grain

applicable_scope: Used for systems where total energy output is distributed among identical particles (e.g., dust grains, stars). Assumes uniform particle properties and steady-state energy emission.

formula_name: 能量吸收与反射平衡公式 Energy Absorption and Reflection Balance

E_{inc}.description: Incident energy falling onto the object

E_{refl}.description: Reflected energy from the object's surface

applicable_scope: Describes the energy balance for a body exposed to incident radiation, taking into account reflection. Typically used in planetary energy budget calculations for atmosphereless or simple atmospheres.

formula_name: 能量与陨石坑直径的关系公式 Crater Excavation Energy-Diameter Relationship

E.description: Excavation energy required to create a crater of diameter D

applicable_scope: Empirical scaling relationship for estimating impact energies from crater diameters on planetary surfaces; applicable primarily to terrestrial planets and rocky bodies under typical gravity and surface conditions.

formula_name: 能量-震级关系式 Energy-Magnitude Relation

formula_name: 能量总和公式 Total Energy Formula

formula_name: 能源效率公式 Energy Conversion Efficiency Formula

formula_name: 牛顿第二定律 Newton's Second Law

formula_name: 牛顿引力场表面重力加速度公式 Newton's Surface Gravitational Acceleration Formula

M.description: Mass of the celestial body (e.g., planet)

formula_name: 浓度公式 Concentration Formula

C.description: Concentration of material (mass per unit volume of water)

applicable_scope: Used in planetary astronomy for calculating the concentration of substances (e.g., salts, minerals) in water reservoirs, such as subsurface oceans of icy moons or planets. Applicable when the mass and volume are well-defined and the solution is homogeneous.

formula_name: 欧若拉产生条件 Aurora Formation Condition

applicable_scope: Computing linear distances between astronomical objects or projected positions in a 2D spatial reference for stellar astronomy and positional measurements. Assumes flat geometry and neglects effects such as parallax or relativistic distortions.

formula_name: 喷流高度表达式 Plume Height Formula

h.description: Maximum height reached by the plume

g.description: Gravitational acceleration of the planetary body

formula_name: 喷流高度与初速度关系公式 Plume Height–Initial Velocity Relation

g.description: Gravitational acceleration at the planetary surface where the plume is generated

formula_name: 膨胀体积关系 Expansion Volume Relation

applicable_scope: Describes how the physical volume of a region in the universe changes with expansion, typically using the cosmological scale factor. Assumes homogeneous and isotropic expansion governed by general relativity.

formula_name: 膨胀宇宙尺度因子演化 Inflationary Scale Factor Evolution Law

formula_name: 膨胀宇宙哈勃常数 Inflationary Universe Hubble Constant

formula_name: 碰撞能量归一化参数 Collision Energy Normalization

formula_name: 屏蔽剂量缩减公式 Shielding Dose Reduction Formula

formula_name: 屏蔽路径积分公式 Shielding Path Integral Formula

formula_name: 屏蔽削弱因子公式 Shielding Reduction Factor Formula

formula_name: 坡度梯度公式 Stream Gradient Formula

formula_name: 坡面角度与梯度关系 Angle-Gradient Relation

formula_name: 曝光亮度累积公式 Exposure Brightness Accumulation Formula

formula_name: 曝光时间与极限星等关系公式 Exposure Time and Limiting Magnitude Relation

formula_name: 期望时间间隔公式 Expected Time Between Events Formula

formula_name: 期望数量公式 Expected Number Formula

formula_name: 全天拼图数公式 Number of Sky Tiles Formula

formula_name: 日冕洞与开放磁力线关系公式 Relation of Coronal Hole to Open Magnetic Field Lines
applicable_scope: This is a descriptive relation expressing that coronal holes on a star, such as the Sun, are regions where the magnetic field lines are open and extend outward into the interplanetary space. There is no single mathematical formula universally representing this relation, but models typically associate the area of coronal holes with the total flux of open magnetic field lines. Physical modelling is used in solar physics for mapping the magnetic topology of coronal holes and understanding their role in solar wind acceleration.

formula_name: 卫星轨道衰减公式 Satellite Orbital Decay Formula

a.description: Semi-major axis of the satellite's orbit

A.description: Cross-sectional area of the satellite

M.description: Mass of the primary body (e.g., planet)

rho.description: Atmospheric density at the orbital altitude

applicable_scope: Describes the rate of change of the semi-major axis for low Earth orbit satellites due to atmospheric drag. Assumes the orbit is sufficiently circular and atmospheric density is uniform at the orbital altitude. Not applicable to orbits where non-atmospheric perturbations dominate.

formula_name: 卫星通道容量与频道数量关系公式 Satellite Transponder Channel Capacity Formula

Total channels.description: Total available communication channels on a satellite system

N_{transponders}.description: Number of transponders equipped on the satellite

n_{channels~per~transponder}.description: Number of communication channels managed by each transponder

applicable_scope: Applicable for counting the total communication channels in satellite hardware architectures. Assumes all transponders are active and have equal channel capacity. Not suitable for cases with non-uniform or degraded transponder performance.

formula_name: 卫星圆形轨道周期公式 Orbital Period of a Circular Satellite

R.description: Radius of the central body (e.g., planet)

h.description: Altitude above the surface of the central body

M.description: Mass of the central body

applicable_scope: This formula applies to satellites in stable, circular orbits around a spherical central mass in Newtonian gravity, neglecting atmospheric drag and relativistic effects.

formula_name: 卫星逐年递减设备损失累计公式 Cumulative Loss from Annual Transponder Failure Formula

Cumulative Loss.description: Total accumulated fraction or proportion of device (such as transponder) loss over n years

p_{lease}.description: Proportion of the satellite system leased or in service (dimensionless, e.g. fractional ownership or usage)

r.description: Annual failure rate of the device (dimensionless, probability per year)

N.description: Number of devices (transponders) considered initially (dimensionless count)

k.description: Year index in the summation from 1 to n

applicable_scope: Applies to cumulative loss estimation of annually-failing service devices (such as satellite transponders) over a fixed operational period, under the assumption of a constant annual failure rate and fixed initial device count. Assumes no replacements and that each device is considered statistically independent.

formula_name: 物理半径标度公式 Physical Radius Scaling Formula

r.description: Physical radius of the star

x.description: Scaling factor representing multiples of the solar radius

applicable_scope: Applicable in stellar astronomy to scale stellar radii relative to the Sun. Assumes the scaling factor is positive and applies to stars where the radius can be reasonably referenced to the solar radius.

formula_name: 物质主导宇宙尺度因子演化 Matter-dominated Scale Factor Evolution Law

R(t).description: Scale factor of the universe at time t (length dimension, relative size of the universe)

t.description: Cosmic time elapsed since the Big Bang

rho.description: Mass-energy density of matter in the universe

applicable_scope: Applicable to homogeneous, isotropic universe models dominated by pressureless matter (dust), and neglects radiation, dark energy or curvature. Based on the Friedmann equations under the assumption of matter domination.

formula_name: 物质主导宇宙哈勃常数 Matter-dominated Universe Hubble Constant

H.description: Hubble constant; the rate of cosmic expansion at time t

t.description: Cosmic time since the Big Bang

applicable_scope: Describes the Hubble parameter for a spatially flat, matter-dominated universe in the standard cosmological model (Friedmann equations, no cosmological constant, negligible radiation).

formula_name: 吸积盘气体温度与距离公式 Accretion Disk Gas Temperature-Distance Formula

T.description: Temperature of the accretion disk gas

M.description: Mass of the central object (e.g., star, black hole)

R.description: Radial distance from the center to the location in the disk

applicable_scope: This formula estimates the characteristic temperature of the gas in an accretion disk surrounding a compact object based on its mass and distance from the center. It is applicable in the context of high-energy astrophysical disks, assuming idealized conditions and ignoring detailed radiation transport or disk composition effects.

formula_name: 吸积盘温度公式（从势能推导） Temperature of Accretion Disk (from Potential Energy)

T.description: Temperature of the accretion disk at radius R

M.description: Mass of the central object (e.g., star, black hole)

R.description: Radial distance from the center of mass (disk radius where temperature is evaluated)

applicable_scope: Applicable for estimating the temperature of a thin accretion disk surrounding a compact object under the assumption of complete potential energy conversion into thermal energy. Assumes non-relativistic particles.

formula_name: 希格斯粒子质量与势能极值 Higgs Mass from Potential Minimum

M.description: Mass of the Higgs particle

x_{\\min}.description: Field value at the minimum of the potential

applicable_scope: Applies to models where the Higgs particle mass is proportional to the minimum of the scalar field potential in cosmological contexts; assumes standard quantum field theory conventions for Higgs mass generation.

formula_name: 希格斯势能函数 Higgs Potential Function

V(x).description: Potential energy as a function of x

x.description: Dimensionless field variable (often normalization of the Higgs field amplitude)

T.description: Reduced temperature parameter (dimensionless, related to thermal history of the universe)

applicable_scope: Used in cosmology and particle physics to model spontaneous symmetry breaking and phase transitions in the early universe. Valid under quantum field theoretical treatment of scalar fields. Specific form applies when considering temperature-dependent modifications to the Higgs potential.

formula_name: 显著性阈值与像素显示公式 Significance Threshold & Pixel Display Formula

N_{\\text{threshold}}.description: The pixel count or signal value corresponding to the significance threshold

N_{\\text{max}}.description: Maximum pixel count or observed signal in the dataset

x.description: Threshold percentage value, given in percent

applicable_scope: Used in the analysis of astronomical images or data to set detection/significance thresholds for planetary objects or features; assumes linear scaling and that counts are positive integers.

formula_name: 相对变化率公式 Relative Rate of Change Formula

L.description: Luminosity or total radiant power emitted by the star

R.description: Radius of the star

applicable_scope: This formula describes the relative rates of change in stellar luminosity, radius, and temperature for stars approximated as blackbodies. It is derived from the Stefan-Boltzmann law under the assumption of small differentials and is applicable to situations where the star's structure is not undergoing drastic non-equilibrium transformations.

formula_name: 相对光速百分比公式 Fraction of Light Speed Formula

Percentage.description: The ratio of an object's speed to the speed of light, expressed as a percentage

applicable_scope: Applicable to scenarios where object's motion is non-relativistic or mildly relativistic within stellar astrophysics, typically used to compare the object's speed to the speed of light.

formula_name: 相对时间膨胀比 Ratio of Gravitational Time Dilation

T_{space}.description: Elapsed time interval measured far from a gravitational source (spacetime unaffected by the body's gravity)

T_{surface}.description: Elapsed time interval measured on the surface of the gravitating body (strong gravitational field)

Ratio.description: Relative time dilation factor between a remote observer and an observer at the surface

applicable_scope: Compares proper time intervals due to gravitational effects as predicted by General Relativity; valid for static, spherically symmetric mass distributions under the Schwarzschild metric or similar spacetime solution.

formula_name: 相机视场宽度公式 Camera Field of View Formula

N_{pixels}.description: Number of pixels per side of the camera sensor (dimensionless)

\\theta_{pixel}.description: Angular width covered by each pixel

\\text{FOV}_{side}.description: Total angular field of view along one side of the camera sensor

\\text{FOV}_{diagonal}.description: Angular field of view along the diagonal of the camera sensor

applicable_scope: Applicable for imaging systems using regular rectangular sensor arrays; assumes uniform pixel angular size and no optical distortion. Commonly used to calculate the observational coverage for astronomical telescopes and cameras for planetary astronomy.

formula_name: 相交圆的公共面积公式 Common Area of Overlapping Circles Formula

A.description: Common overlapping area between two identical circles under specific geometric arrangement

R.description: Radius of either circle

applicable_scope: This formula applies to the case where two identical circles overlap such that the intersecting area is symmetric and determined by their relative positions. Used in planetary astronomy for modeling projection and occultation phenomena involving circular disks (e.g., planetary transits or eclipses).

formula_name: 相同质量下球体半径与数量的关系 Radius-Number Relation for Equal Mass Spheres

r_N.description: Radius of a single sphere when the total mass is divided into N equal-mass spheres

r_1.description: Radius of the original single sphere (before division)

N.description: Number of equal-mass spheres

applicable_scope: Applicable when a total mass of a sphere is redistributed into N identical smaller spheres, assuming uniform density and composition. Used for physical and planetary fragmentation or distribution models.

formula_name: 向量夹角速度差公式 Velocity Change at Angle (Law of Cosines for Velocity Vectors)

V_{d}.description: Magnitude of velocity difference between two vectors separated by 60 degrees

V_{f}.description: Magnitude of the initial velocity vector

applicable_scope: Applies to velocity vector calculations in planetary astronomy, where the angle between two velocity vectors is 60 degrees. Based on the law of cosines for vectors; relevant when analyzing flybys or orbital encounters involving known vector orientations.

formula_name: 向心力公式 Centripetal Force Formula

F_c.description: Centripetal force acting on the body

m.description: Mass of the object in motion

V.description: Tangential velocity of the object

R.description: Radius of the circular path

applicable_scope: Describes the required force for an object of mass m moving at velocity V to maintain a circular orbit of radius R in planetary orbits or rotating systems, under the assumption of uniform circular motion and non-relativistic speeds (V << c).

formula_name: 信号往返时间公式 Round-Trip Signal Time

t_{\\text{round}}.description: Total round-trip time taken by a signal to travel to a target and return

d.description: One-way distance between signal source and target

applicable_scope: Applicable for two-way ranging or communication between astronomical bodies (e.g., radar measurements or spacecraft tracking) where the signal propagates at a constant speed and relativistic effects are negligible.

formula_name: 信噪比提升定律 Signal-to-Noise Improvement Law

SNR.description: Signal-to-noise ratio; a dimensionless measure of signal strength relative to noise

N.description: Number of independent measurements (e.g., exposures or samples); dimensionless count

applicable_scope: Applicable to cases where noise is random and uncorrelated between measurements, such as photon noise in stellar photometry. Assumes the improvement follows the square root law typical for uncorrelated, Gaussian noise.

formula_name: 星等差（线性）公式 Magnitude Difference (Linear) Formula

\\Delta m.description: Difference in apparent magnitude between two astronomical objects

m_1.description: Apparent magnitude of the first astronomical object

m_2.description: Apparent magnitude of the second astronomical object

applicable_scope: Applicable to any comparison of apparent magnitudes in stellar astronomy, where the magnitude scale is valid and both objects are measured in the same photometric system.

formula_name: 星等差-亮度关系对数表达式 Magnitude Difference-Brightness Ratio Logarithmic Expression
applicable_scope: Relates magnitude difference and brightness ratio for astronomical objects observed in the same bandpass; applicable to photometric measurements within the logarithmic magnitude system for stellar flux.

formula_name: 星等-流量关系 Magnitude–Flux Relation

S(m).description: Flux density corresponding to magnitude m (measured power per unit area)

S_0.description: Reference flux density, usually corresponding to zero magnitude

m.description: Apparent magnitude of the astronomical object

applicable_scope: Applicable for the measurement of apparent magnitudes and fluxes of astronomical objects under the standard definition of magnitude. Assumes linear detector response and photometric calibration.

formula_name: 星等星数关系公式 Relationship between Star Count and Magnitude

N(m).description: Number of stars brighter than apparent magnitude m

m.description: Apparent magnitude, a logarithmic measure of stellar brightness

C.description: Empirical constant determined by sky region and survey depth

applicable_scope: Statistical relationship valid for uniform, non-crowded stellar fields and in surveys where completeness is well-controlled. Applies to regions where interstellar extinction and selection effects are negligible.

formula_name: 星等与亮度公式 Magnitude-Brightness Relation

B.description: Observed flux or brightness of the star

m.description: Apparent magnitude of the astronomical object

applicable_scope: Relates the apparent magnitude to observed flux under the logarithmic astronomical magnitude system; applicable for non-variable, point-like astronomical sources.

formula_name: 星等至光度关系公式 Luminosity-Flux Relation

m_1.description: Apparent magnitude of the first star (dimensionless logarithmic scale)

m_2.description: Apparent magnitude of the second star (dimensionless logarithmic scale)

F_1.description: Observed flux from the first star (energy per unit area per unit time)

F_2.description: Observed flux from the second star (energy per unit area per unit time)

applicable_scope: Relates the difference in apparent magnitudes of two stars to their observed fluxes. Valid when observations are made in the same photometric band and atmospheric effects are negligible.

formula_name: 星际介质质量组成公式 Interstellar Medium Mass Composition Formula

M_{\\text{ISM}}.description: Total mass of the interstellar medium (gas and dust) in a region

M_{\\text{dust}}.description: Mass of dust contained in the interstellar medium

f_{\\text{dust}}.description: Fractional mass abundance of dust in the interstellar medium (dust-to-ISM mass ratio)

applicable_scope: This formula estimates the total interstellar medium mass from measured dust mass and dust mass fraction. Applicable under the assumption of well-mixed dust and gas, and when the dust-to-ISM mass ratio can be reliably determined. Often used in studies of star formation regions and stellar environments.

formula_name: 星际介质总质量和比例公式 ISM Total Mass and Fraction Formula

M_{\\text{ISM}}.description: Total mass of the interstellar medium (gas and dust) in a given region

M_{\\text{dust}}.description: Total mass of dust present in the interstellar medium

applicable_scope: Estimates of the total interstellar medium (ISM) mass in galaxies, assuming a typical dust-to-gas mass ratio of ~0.01; appropriate for Milky Way-like environments outside of low metallicity regions.

formula_name: 星面过渡掩食亮度公式 Transit Depth Formula

Delta F.description: Decrease in observed stellar flux during planetary transit

F.description: Total observed stellar flux without transit

applicable_scope: Used in exoplanet transit observations under the assumption of a small planet compared to the star and a uniform stellar disk.

formula_name: 星体潮汐破裂距离近似公式 Tidal Disruption Distance Formula

r.description: Tidal disruption radius; the distance from the black hole where a star will be tidally disrupted

R.description: Radius of the star

M.description: Mass of the black hole

applicable_scope: Valid for estimating the tidal disruption distance for a star approaching a massive black hole; assumes the star is treated as a point mass and disruption occurs when tidal forces exceed stellar self-gravity. Relativistic effects are neglected.

formula_name: 星系旋转曲线经验公式 Empirical Formula for Galaxy Rotation Curve

V.description: Orbital velocity of stars or gas at distance x from the galactic center

x.description: Radial distance from the center of the galaxy (typically in kiloparsecs, but base SI unit is meter)

applicable_scope: This empirical formula describes the typical rotation curve of disk galaxies, modeling the decreasing velocity at large radii. It is used for qualitative analysis and does not derive from first principles. The formula is valid only for positions away from the very center (x > 0) where disk rotation can be observed.

formula_name: 星系旋转速度函数 Galaxy Rotation Velocity Function

V.description: Circular rotation velocity of the galaxy at a scaled radius

x.description: Scaled radial coordinate (usually ratio of radius to a characteristic scale length)

applicable_scope: Describes the theoretical rotation curve of a galaxy as a function of a scaled radius parameter. Specific physical interpretation of x and the velocity normalization may depend on model assumptions (e.g., mass distribution, dark matter halo profile).

formula_name: 星云介质总质量估算公式 Total Mass Estimate Formula for Nebular Medium

M_{\\text{ISM,total}}.description: Total mass of the interstellar medium (ISM), including both dust and gas

M_{\\text{dust}}.description: Mass of dust present in the nebular medium

f_{\\text{dust}}.description: Dust mass fraction; the ratio of dust mass to total ISM mass

applicable_scope: This formula estimates the total mass of the interstellar medium when the dust mass and dust-to-ISM ratio are known. Valid in regions where the composition of the nebula follows standard ISM properties and dust fraction is constant.

formula_name: 行星表面平衡温度估算式 Planetary Equilibrium Temperature Estimation

T.description: Estimated equilibrium temperature of the planet's surface

R.description: Orbital distance of the planet from the central star (usually the Sun); astronomical unit

applicable_scope: Empirical estimation of planetary surface temperature based on distance from the Sun; assumes the planet behaves as a blackbody, receives solar flux, and neglects atmospheric or albedo effects. Valid for main sequence stars similar to Sun and in absence of other significant heat sources.

formula_name: 行星测量半径公式 Planetary Radius on Drawing

r_{\\text{planet drawing}}.description: Radius of the planet on the drawing or map

R_{\\text{planet}}.description: True physical radius of the planet

Scale.description: Map or drawing scale factor, defined as (real length)/(drawing length)

applicable_scope: Used for calculating the representative size of a planet on an astronomical map or diagram based on its actual radius and the applied scale factor. Only valid for linear scales and assumes accurate input data.

formula_name: 行星尺度与面积计算公式 Formula for Area Calculation of a Square Field

Area.description: The total area of the square field

side length.description: The length of one side of the square field

applicable_scope: Applicable for calculating projected area of square fields in astronomical surveys; assumes perfect square geometry and Euclidean space.

formula_name: 行星大气屏蔽总质量公式 Total Atmospheric Column Mass Formula

D.description: Total atmospheric column mass per unit area (surface density) above a given point

N(0).description: Particle number density at the surface (z = 0)

H.description: Scale height of the atmosphere, representing the height over which the atmospheric pressure decreases by a factor of e

applicable_scope: Applicable to isothermal, hydrostatic equilibrium atmospheres. Assumes ideal gas conditions and negligible variation of composition or gravity with altitude.

formula_name: 行星公转周期 Orbital Period of Planet

T.description: Orbital period of the planet in seconds

P.description: Integer numerator representing the period as a fraction

Q.description: Integer denominator representing the period as a fraction

applicable_scope: Used for expressing the orbital period of a planet as a rational fraction, usually referenced to Earth years and convertible to seconds for SI calculation. Suitable for simplified resonance relations and approximate period ratios between planetary orbits.

formula_name: 行星平衡温度公式 Planetary Equilibrium Temperature Formula

A.description: Bond albedo of the planet (fraction of incident starlight reflected)

L.description: Luminosity of the host star

D.description: Distance from the planet to the star (orbital radius)

applicable_scope: Estimates the mean surface temperature of a planet assuming perfect energy balance between absorbed starlight and radiated infrared energy; neglects greenhouse effects, internal heating, and non-blackbody factors. Valid for exoplanet or Solar System planet temperature calculations.

formula_name: 行星热流总功率公式 Planetary Total Heat Output Formula

P.description: Total heat output power of the planet

F.description: Surface heat flux (thermal energy flow per unit surface area)

S.description: Surface area through which the heat flux passes

applicable_scope: Used to calculate the total thermal power output from a planetary body assuming a constant and isotropic surface heat flux. The formula is valid for planetary objects with a well-defined surface.

formula_name: 行星有效温度公式 Planetary Effective Temperature Formula

T_p.description: Effective temperature of the planet due to stellar irradiation

d.description: Distance between the star and the planet

A.description: Bond albedo of the planet (fraction of stellar energy reflected)

applicable_scope: Estimates the equilibrium temperature of a planet assuming it is a perfect blackbody, in radiative equilibrium with its parent star; does not include atmospheric or internal heating effects.

formula_name: 行星质量增长率公式 Planet Mass Accretion Rate Formula

dM/dt.description: Mass accretion rate; rate of increase of planet's mass

R.description: Radius of the accreting planet

m.description: Mass of an individual accreted particle

N.description: Number density of accreted particles

V.description: Relative velocity between the planet and the accreted medium

applicable_scope: Describes the rate at which a planet accretes mass within a gaseous or particulate medium under the assumption of uniform density and velocity distribution. Valid for early stage planetary growth models.

formula_name: 悬挂点质量守恒原理 Suspension Point Mass Conservation Principle

M_{\\text{total}}.description: Total mass of the system at the suspension point

m_i.description: Individual mass of the i-th component within the system

constraints: Number of components (i) is finite and positive
applicable_scope: Applicable in closed or isolated planetary systems where mass transfer, mass loss, or accretion is negligible on relevant timescales. The principle is based on simple mass conservation and assumes no significant external exchange of matter.

formula_name: 旋转体体积公式（圆盘/柱壳法） Volume of Solid of Revolution (Disk/Shell Method)

V.description: Volume of the solid formed by revolving region around the axis

x.description: Radial coordinate from the axis of rotation

f(x).description: Function describing the distance from axis of rotation (radius) as a function of x

constraints: f(x) >= 0 for all x in [a, b]
applicable_scope: Used to compute volumes of axisymmetric celestial bodies such as planets, asteroids, or other solids by revolving a profile curve. Applicable when symmetry around an axis is present and boundaries a, b are physically meaningful.

formula_name: 旋转周期求法（根据距离与时间） Rotation Period from Linear Speed

R.description: Radius from the rotation axis to the measurement point

applicable_scope: Applicable for objects exhibiting rigid body rotation at radius R, where tangential speed v is constant. Assumes negligible differential rotation and non-relativistic speeds.

formula_name: 沿地表距离公式 Distance Along the Surface Formula

L.description: Distance along the surface of a planet between an observer at height h and a point directly below

R.description: Mean radius of the planet or celestial body

h.description: Observer's height above the surface of the planet

applicable_scope: Applicable for calculating the ground distance visible from an elevated point above the surface of a spherical planet. Assumes a perfect sphere and neglects atmospheric effects.

formula_name: 沿地表距离公式的导数 Derivative of Distance Along the Surface Formula

L.description: Distance along the planetary surface measured from the point vertically above the surface.

h.description: Height above the planetary surface from which the distance is measured.

R.description: Mean radius of the planet.

applicable_scope: Applicable to planetary bodies when calculating the derivative of distance along the planetary surface as seen from a point above the surface. Assumes the planet is a perfect sphere and neglects local topographic features and atmospheric effects.

formula_name: 氧原子质量与夸克质量比 Mass Ratio: Oxygen Atom to Top Quark

N.description: Dimensionless mass ratio of one oxygen atom to one top quark

applicable_scope: Used for comparative mass scaling between atomic and subatomic particles in calculations of stellar processes and nuclear reactions; physical constants valid under standard conditions in particle physics.

formula_name: 以质量表示的质量增长率方程 Mass Accretion Rate in Terms of Mass

M.description: Current mass of the accreting body

N.description: Number density of accreted particles

V.description: Relative velocity between body and accreted particles

applicable_scope: Describes mass growth rate of a body due to accretion in a particle-rich environment, assuming spherical symmetry and negligible self-gravity of individual particles. Relevant for early planet formation and dust/gas accretion.

formula_name: 引力红移公式 Gravitational Redshift Formula

W.description: The observed wavelength after gravitational redshift

V.description: Gravitational potential difference (typically GM/R)

R.description: Reference length scale (e.g., radius at which potential is evaluated)

applicable_scope: Applicable for calculating the gravitational redshift of spectral lines at or near the surface of massive bodies such as stars. Assumes non-relativistic limit and small potential difference (V << c^2). Wavelength measured in nanometres.

formula_name: 引力势能公式 Gravitational Potential Energy Formula

P.E..description: Gravitational potential energy between two masses

M.description: Mass of the primary object (e.g., star)

m.description: Mass of the secondary object (e.g., orbiting body, particle)

R.description: Separation between the centers of the two masses

applicable_scope: Applicable for calculating the gravitational potential energy in stellar systems under Newtonian gravity; ignores relativistic corrections, valid when R is much larger than the Schwarzschild radius of M.

formula_name: 引力势能与动能守恒公式 Gravitational Potential and Kinetic Energy Conservation Formula

m.description: Mass of the object (e.g., star, planet, or particle)

V.description: Velocity of the object

M.description: Mass of the central body (e.g., star, planet)

R.description: Separation distance between the object and the center of mass of M

applicable_scope: Valid for the conservation of energy in isolated gravitational two-body systems, where only gravitational forces are considered and relativistic effects are negligible.

formula_name: 引力主导形状极限(重力-物质刚性平衡) Gravitational-Rigidity (Hydrostatic Equilibrium) Limit

M.description: Total mass of the astronomical object

R.description: Effective radius of the object

Y.description: Shear modulus (rigidity) of the object's material

mu.description: Mean molecular mass of the object's material

applicable_scope: Determines the minimum size or mass at which an object's gravity overcomes its material rigidity, leading to a nearly spherical shape; generally applies to large planetary bodies where self-gravity dominates over internal rigidity.

formula_name: 英里每千米转换公式 Miles to Kilometers Conversion Formula

mile.description: Distance in miles (converted to meters for SI base unit)

km.description: Distance in kilometers (converted to meters for SI base unit)

applicable_scope: Unit conversion between imperial and metric systems, used in astronomical distance measurements, stellar cataloging, and observation reporting. Valid for positive distances only.

formula_name: 泳斑周期与太阳活动相关性公式 Sunspot Cycle and Solar Activity Correlation Formula

N_{ss}.description: Sunspot number; dimensionless count of observed sunspots

\\phi_{cycle}.description: Phase of the solar cycle; normalized phase between 0 (minimum) and 1 (maximum)

\\text{Number of Solar Flares}.description: Count of solar flares occurring within a specified time interval

applicable_scope: Applicable for statistical correlations between sunspot numbers and solar flare occurrences within an active solar cycle phase; empirical relationship valid for main-sequence solar-type stars similar to the Sun.

formula_name: 用质量表示的质量增长率公式 Mass-Based Mass Growth Rate Formula

M.description: Total mass of the accreting body (e.g., proto-planet)

m.description: Mass of individual accreted particle (e.g., planetesimal, dust grain)

N.description: Number density of the accreted particles

V.description: Mean relative velocity between accretor and accreted particles

rho.description: Bulk density of the accreting body

applicable_scope: Applies to the planetesimal or proto-planetary growth via accretion of small bodies (particle swarm accretion); validity assumes uniform particle distribution, non-zero impact velocity, and spherical symmetry for mass-radius relation.

formula_name: 有效能量释放 Efficiency-Corrected Energy Release

E.description: Amount of energy released

m.description: Rest mass of the object or fuel involved

\\eta.description: Efficiency factor; fraction of rest mass energy converted

applicable_scope: Used to estimate total energy release in astrophysical processes where not all mass is converted (e.g., nuclear fusion in stars, accretion disks); actual efficiency depends on the physical mechanism.

formula_name: 宇宙参数关系 Friedmann Equation (Flat Universe Example)

H(z).description: Hubble parameter at redshift z, representing the expansion rate of the Universe

z.description: Cosmological redshift, representing the fractional change in wavelength due to cosmic expansion

Omega_m.description: Present-day matter density parameter (fraction of critical density contributed by matter)

Omega_Lambda.description: Cosmological constant/dark energy density parameter (fraction of critical density contributed by dark energy)

applicable_scope: Applies to flat Lambda-CDM cosmological models assuming general relativity. The sum of density parameters equals one: Omega_m + Omega_Lambda = 1. Valid for homogeneous and isotropic universes and does not include contributions from radiation or curvature.

formula_name: 宇宙红移与观测红移 Cosmological Redshift

z.description: Cosmological redshift, dimensionless

applicable_scope: Describes the shift in observed wavelength due to cosmological expansion; applicable to large-scale extragalactic and cosmological observations where spacetime is expanding and special relativistic effects are negligible compared to the expansion.

formula_name: 宇宙年龄 Cosmic Age Formula

t_0.description: The age of the universe (cosmic time elapsed since the Big Bang)

z.description: Redshift parameter as the variable of integration

H_0.description: Hubble constant; current expansion rate of the universe

Omega_\\Lambda.description: Present-day normalized cosmological constant (dark energy density parameter)

applicable_scope: This formula applies to a flat homogeneous and isotropic universe described by the ΛCDM model with normalized density parameters for matter and dark energy. It assumes no significant contribution from radiation or curvature.

formula_name: 宇宙年龄与回望时间 Look-back Time Formula

t_L.description: Look-back time, the time difference between the emission and observation of light from a distant object

z.description: Redshift, quantifies the observed wavelength shift due to cosmic expansion

z'.description: Dummy variable of integration representing redshift

Omega_m.description: Density parameter of matter in the universe

Omega_\\Lambda.description: Density parameter of dark energy (cosmological constant)

applicable_scope: Applicable for homogeneous and isotropic cosmological models (Friedmann-Lemaître-Robertson-Walker metric) in a flat or curved universe with matter and dark energy contributions. Assumes standard cosmological parameterization and ignores radiation density for low to moderate redshift.

formula_name: 宇宙膨胀倍率公式 Cosmic Scale Factor (Dilation Factor) Formula

a.description: Cosmic scale factor (dimensionless), representing the relative expansion of the universe between two epochs

d_{\\text{final}}.description: Physical distance between two points at the final epoch

d_{\\text{initial}}.description: Physical distance between the same two points at the initial epoch

applicable_scope: Applicable to measurements and models of cosmic expansion under the Friedmann–Lemaître–Robertson–Walker metric; assumes homogeneous and isotropic universe.

formula_name: 宇宙膨胀时距离缩放公式 Distance Scaling in Expanding Universe

d_{\\text{then}}.description: Proper physical distance between two points at an earlier epoch ('then')

d_{\\text{now}}.description: Proper physical distance between the same two points at the current epoch ('now')

S.description: Cosmic scale factor (ratio of expansion between 'then' and 'now')

applicable_scope: Relates proper distances at different cosmic epochs under the FLRW metric in standard cosmological models, assuming homogeneity and isotropy. Applicable for large-scale structure evolution and any epoch where the scale factor is well-defined.

formula_name: 宇宙温度-时间关系公式 Temperature-Time Relation in the Early Universe

T.description: Temperature of the universe at time t

t.description: Time elapsed since the Big Bang

applicable_scope: Applicable during the radiation-dominated era of the early universe (t < 10^12 s). Assumes homogeneous and isotropic universe governed by standard cosmology, ignores later matter and dark energy effects.

formula_name: 宇宙早期粒子平均能量公式 Early Universe Particle Mean Energy Equation

E.description: Mean energy of a particle in the early universe

t.description: Cosmic time since the Big Bang

applicable_scope: Applicable to the estimation of particle average energy at a given time in the early universe, under the assumption of a hot, dense, and rapidly expanding cosmological plasma. Only valid when t is in the range where the universe behaves as an ideal radiation-dominated plasma. Energy must be converted from eV to Joules for SI calculations.

formula_name: 宇宙早期温度演化公式 Early Universe Temperature Evolution Equation

T.description: Temperature of the universe at time t after the Big Bang

t.description: Elapsed time since the Big Bang

applicable_scope: Describes the approximate temperature evolution of the universe during its early stages, under the standard cosmological model when the universe is radiation dominated (for t << 1 year). Assumes negligible contributions from matter and cosmological constant, suitable for times after the Planck epoch and before recombination.

formula_name: 元素质量分数计算公式 Atmospheric Constituent Mass Fraction Formula

m_i.description: Mass of the i-th atmospheric constituent

M_{atm}.description: Total mass of the planetary atmosphere

f_i.description: Mass fraction of the i-th atmospheric constituent

applicable_scope: Calculating the mass of individual chemical or isotopic constituents in a planetary atmosphere based on total mass and known mass fraction. Assumes all fractions are correctly normalized and species are distinguishable.

formula_name: 原子数密度公式 Atomic Number Density Formula

N.description: Atomic number density; number of atoms per unit volume

D.description: Mass density of the material

M.description: Total mass of the material

V.description: Volume occupied by the material

applicable_scope: Applicable to the estimation of atomic number density in stellar interiors or astrophysical plasmas where a single dominant atomic species is assumed and the material is evenly mixed.

formula_name: 原子质量加和法 Atomic Mass Summation

n_i.description: Number of atoms of type i

Total atomic mass.description: Combined atomic mass of the system

applicable_scope: Used to calculate the total mass of a system by summing the masses of constituent atomic species, notably applied in stellar composition studies where different elements' abundances are considered.

formula_name: 圆柱体体积公式 Volume of Cylinder

V.description: Volume of the cylinder

R.description: Radius of the base of the cylinder

h.description: Height of the cylinder

applicable_scope: Geometrical calculation of cylindrical volumes, often used in estimating the volume of planetary structures or laboratory sample holders in planetary astronomy. Assumes idealized perfect cylinder shape.

formula_name: 圆锥体体积公式 Cone Volume Formula

V.description: Volume of the cone

R.description: Radius of the base of the cone

applicable_scope: Geometric calculation of volume for conical features of planetary bodies, such as crater shapes or artificial constructs

formula_name: 远距离小角度近似 Small Angle Approximation

theta.description: Angular separation in radians

D.description: Physical size or separation between two objects

d.description: Distance from observer to the object

applicable_scope: Valid for small angles (typically <~0.1 radians) and distant objects, used in resolving angular sizes/distances in planetary and observational astronomy.

formula_name: 远日点公式 Aphelion Distance Formula

r_a.description: Aphelion distance; the farthest orbital distance between an object and the focal point (usually the Sun)

a.description: Semi-major axis of the orbital ellipse

e.description: Orbital eccentricity; describes the deviation from circularity (0 = circle, < 1 = ellipse)

applicable_scope: Valid for Keplerian orbits of planets, asteroids, or comets around a dominant central body; applies in the absence of strong perturbations or relativistic corrections.

formula_name: 远日点距离公式 Aphelion Distance Formula

r_{a}.description: Aphelion distance, the maximum separation between an orbiting body and its focus (usually the Sun).

e.description: Orbital eccentricity, dimensionless parameter describing shape of the orbit.

applicable_scope: Describes the furthest orbital point for planets or minor bodies on elliptical orbits around a central mass where Newtonian gravity applies. Valid for bound two-body Keplerian orbits.

formula_name: 陨石坑表面密度（单位面积密度） Areal Crater Density

A_c.description: Areal crater density, or number of craters per unit area

N.description: Total number of craters counted

A.description: Surveyed surface area

applicable_scope: Applicable to planetary surfaces where crater counts are used to estimate geologic age or resurfacing rates, assuming craters are counted accurately within a well-defined area.

formula_name: 陨石坑表面密度函数 Crater Surface Density Function

S.description: Surface density of impact craters of diameter greater than x

x.description: Crater diameter threshold

applicable_scope: Statistical estimation of impact crater densities on planetary surfaces, typically valid for planetary bodies with sufficiently large numbers of craters and within the observational size range (e.g., lunar or Martian craters).

formula_name: 陨石坑平均间距公式 Mean Crater Spacing Formula

d.description: Mean spacing between impact craters

A_c.description: Crater density, defined as number of craters per square meter

applicable_scope: Applicable for analyzing planetary surfaces covered with randomly distributed impact craters, assuming statistical uniformity of crater distribution.

formula_name: 载荷比公式 Payload Ratio Formula

Payload Ratio.description: The ratio between the required fuel mass and the maximum cargo mass of a spacecraft.

m_{fuel,needed}.description: The mass of propellant required for the mission.

m_{cargo,max}.description: The maximum mass allowed for payload or cargo.

applicable_scope: Relevant to spacecraft engineering and mission planning when calculating fuel requirements relative to payload capacity, typically under Newtonian mechanics and neglecting relativistic effects or mass loss by other processes.

formula_name: 长度单位换算公式 Length Unit Conversion Formula

L_{\\text{mm}}.description: Length in millimeters (expressed in SI unit meters for calculation; 1 mm = 1e-3 m)

L_{\\text{in}}.description: Length in inches (expressed in SI unit meters for calculation; 1 in = 0.0254 m)

applicable_scope: Direct conversion between inch and millimeter lengths for astrometric and planetary measurements; both should be interpreted in SI unit meters for computation.

formula_name: 照相测量恒星视差公式 Stellar Parallax Formula

d.description: Distance to the star

applicable_scope: Applicable for determining the distances to nearby stars using the stellar parallax method. Accurate for small parallax angles and assumes a direct observation baseline from one astronomical unit.

formula_name: 遮蔽面积百分比公式 Fractional Area Occulted (Transit Depth)

Percent Reduction.description: Percentage of stellar disk obscured by the planet during a transit

A_{planet}.description: Projected area of the planet's disk

A_{star}.description: Projected area of the star's disk

applicable_scope: Used during planetary transits to calculate transit depth for exoplanet or eclipse studies. Assumes the planet passes directly in front of the star as seen by an observer; neglects limb darkening and assumes circular disk geometry.

formula_name: 真空能密度势能函数 Vacuum Energy Potential Function

V.description: Potential energy density of the vacuum as a function of x and T

x.description: Dimensionless scalar field variable

applicable_scope: Describes the vacuum energy potential relevant to cosmological phase transitions and models of early-universe inflation. The form is specific to effective field theory approaches; values of T typically reflect symmetry-breaking conditions.

formula_name: 正弦定理 Sine Law

theta.description: Angle subtended or viewing angle

a.description: Length of the side opposite to angle theta

c.description: Length of the hypotenuse or reference side

applicable_scope: Applies to geometric calculations involving viewing angles or position triangles, typically for line-of-sight or angular separation determinations in planetary astronomy. Assumes Euclidean geometry and small-angle approximations are not necessarily required.

formula_name: 指数衰减函数 Exponential Decay Function

R(T).description: Quantity after decay; a dimensionless ratio

T.description: Elapsed time; usually interpreted as system age in gigayears (1 Gyr = 10^9 years). For SI, T in seconds: T[s] = T[Gyr] * 3.15e16

applicable_scope: Used to model the exponential decline of a stellar property (e.g., supernova rate, gas fraction, activity indicator) as a function of stellar age. Only appropriate when the decay is empirically observed and fits an exponential law, and T represents age in gigayears unless otherwise specified.

formula_name: 指数型大气密度分布 Exponential Atmospheric Density Profile

z.description: Altitude above reference level

H.description: Scale height characterizing the exponential pressure decrease

applicable_scope: Applicability to planetary atmospheres under the assumption of isothermal layers and hydrostatic equilibrium. Does not account for temperature gradients, condensation, or molecular composition variations.

formula_name: 指数增长函数 Exponential Growth Function

R(T).description: Exponential function result, context-dependent; may represent a rate or ratio without explicit physical dimension

applicable_scope: Applicable where an exponential dependence on temperature is present in stellar astronomy, for instance, in describing thermally activated processes or approximate reaction rates. The formula is empirical and should only be used when justified by the underlying physical model.

formula_name: 质量-半径关系（球体） Mass-Radius Relation (Sphere)

M(t).description: Mass of the sphere as a function of time

R(t).description: Radius of the sphere as a function of time

\\rho.description: Mass density of the sphere (assumed uniform)

applicable_scope: Applies to homogeneous spheres (e.g., planets, stars) with uniform and time-dependent density, under the assumption of spherical symmetry and no rotation or other mass distribution anomalies in planetary astronomy.

formula_name: 质量-粒子数换算公式 Mass-to-Particle Number Conversion Formula

N.description: Number of particles (dimensionless count)

m_{atom}.description: Mass of a single atom or particle

applicable_scope: Applies to calculating the number of particles in a homogeneous medium given its mass density and particle mass; commonly used in stellar interiors assuming a single atomic species or well-defined mean atomic mass.

formula_name: 质量—粒子数—平均质量关系公式 Mass from Number of Particles and Mean Atomic Mass

m.description: Total mass of the object or material

N.description: Number of particles (typically atoms or molecules)

m_{\\text{atom}}.description: Mean mass per particle (usually atom or molecule)

applicable_scope: Applicable to calculating total mass of a collection of particles (e.g., a planetary atmosphere or crust) when individual particle mass and total particle count are known. Assumes uniform mean atomic/molecular mass.

formula_name: 质量密度公式 Mass from Density and Volume

M.description: Mass of the astronomical object

V.description: Volume occupied by the object

applicable_scope: Applicable for calculating the mass of planetary bodies or other astronomical objects assuming uniform density and a well-defined volume.

applicable_scope: Describes mass growth through accretion (e.g., planetesimal or proto-planet) under continuous collection of small particles. Assumes a uniform background density, point-mass approximation, and validity for early stages of planet formation where cohesion dominates.

formula_name: 质量随时间演化公式 Mass Growth as Function of Time

applicable_scope: Applicable to planetary accretion processes under idealized mass growth by uniform accretion of material, assuming constant density and number density.

formula_name: 质量随时间增长解析式 Explicit Mass Growth with Time

rho.description: Ambient mass density of surrounding medium

formula_name: 质量与半径关系公式 Mass-Radius Relation for Asteroid

M(t).description: Mass of the asteroid at time t

applicable_scope: Valid for asteroids considered as homogeneous spheres; neglects irregular shapes and internal porosity. Applicable in planetary science for determining mass given time-dependent radius and uniform density.

constraints: Energy in GeV must be non-negative
applicable_scope: This conversion is based on special relativity, relating mass and energy via E=mc^2. formula_name: 质量与体积关系公式 Mass-Volume-Density Relationship

applicable_scope: Relates the mass, volume, and density of planetary objects, assuming uniform density and classical conditions.

formula_name: 质能等价公式 Mass-Energy Equivalence

E.description: Energy equivalent of mass

formula_name: 质能方程 Mass-Energy Equivalence

formula_name: 重力加速度与重量关系 Weight-Gravity Acceleration Relation

formula_name: 重力势能公式 Gravitational Potential Energy Formula

P.E..description: Gravitational potential energy of an object

formula_name: 撞击率公式 Impact Rate Formula

formula_name: 自由下落运动公式 Free-Fall Motion Equation
applicable_scope: This formula describes the vertical distance traveled by an object in free fall under constant gravitational acceleration, neglecting atmospheric drag and assuming the initial velocity is zero. Applicable near planetary surfaces where gravity can be treated as uniform.
